checks.finding: T-001, T-006, T-007, and T-008 consistently declare medium for limited, controlled, behavioral changes. T-002 through T-005 consistently declare high for material, difficult, sensitive workflow and trust-boundary changes.

Each high-risk task T-002 through T-005 names red and green evidence paths, requires failing tests committed before implementation, and names a distinct independent reviewer whose PASS must be recorded.
T-001 through T-008 each define one coherent deliverable with bounded Scope, explicit Out of Scope, and no more than six Done When items.

TEST-001 covers unavailable tiers, recurrence variants, forbidden escalation, unavailable runtimes, and invalid resume; TEST-002 and TEST-003 cover identity reuse, missing manifests, traversal, symlinks, mutation, malformed provenance, and tampering; TEST-004 covers malformed current reports; TEST-006 covers forced rollback failure.

The planned tests match their boundaries: TEST-001 exercises routing behavior, TEST-002 and TEST-003 exercise cross-component orchestration/validator integration, TEST-004 validates report contracts, TEST-005 is repository regression, and TEST-006 is an isolated rollback transaction test; each references its governing AC.
Every high-risk task T-002 through T-005 has an explicit Rollback field and a Done When item requiring an isolated rollback fixture for its scoped behavior.

The task scopes separate capability declarations, routing/resume, manifest mechanics, implementation orchestration, review isolation, reporting, release synchronization, and rollback tooling. Shared contracts are consumed through explicit blockers rather than modified concurrently for the same purpose.

Blockers correspond to produced artifacts: T-002 and T-003 consume T-001 tiers; T-004 consumes routing and manifest contracts; T-005 consumes the manifest contract; T-006 consumes orchestration and isolation identities; T-007 waits for all release content; T-008 requires T-007's finalized release hashes.
SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope.
